qa.overall_assessment: BA's core diagnosis and proposed fix are technically sound and well-evidenced. Two issues require BA to update the spec before dev proceeds: (1) AC3 covers only the multi-candidate fail-closed path but omits the single-candidate fail-closed path, leaving a structural gap in coverage; (2) AC4's verification recipe uses a time-transient directory-wide grep that will produce a false-pass once any future spec acquires the marker. Both are fixable with targeted wording changes in the BA spec.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed no blocker on the core predicate change. Two PROPOSED_FIX findings: (1) AC4 verification is time-transient/overbroad — reword to target the known false-positive files rather than the whole specs directory; (2) single-candidate fail-closed path is not explicitly checked by BA — add AC3 verification for the one-element outcome. Two OBSERVATION_ONLY findings: marker-placement enforcement is future-cycle debt (not in scope); line 157 number may drift but the predicate content is the stable anchor.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Both codex PROPOSED_FIX findings are raised as blocking objections (AC4 time-transient verification and AC3 single-candidate gap). The two OBSERVATION_ONLY findings are logged in out_of_scope_observations.

objections.title: AC3 covers only multi-candidate fail-closed path; single-candidate path not verified
objections.detail: Stage 4 of the Step 7 algorithm has TWO fail-closed branches: (a) exactly one candidate → 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context: <path>' + exit non-zero; (b) multiple candidates → 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage: <paths>; explicit context.spec_path required' + exit non-zero. BA's AC3 covers ONLY branch (b) — its GIVEN clause requires 'two spec files' and its verification recipe checks only for the multi-candidate message text. The single-candidate branch (a) is structurally separate and equally affected by the predicate change: if exactly one spec contains the marker, the single-candidate fail-closed branch fires. BA did not add a verification for this path.
objections.evidence: AC3 text in ticket-20260522-080646-C.md lines 147-153: GIVEN requires two spec files; verification grep targets 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage' only. commands/commit.md line 161 confirms the single-candidate branch exists: 'If set has exactly one element: print spec produced this cycle but not linked in context: <path> and exit non-zero (fail-closed)'.
objections.proposed_fix: Extend AC3 to cover both fail-closed branches. Add a sub-clause: 'GIVEN a spec file containing the marker AND stage 3 produces exactly one candidate, THEN the single-candidate outcome fires: print spec produced this cycle but not linked in context: <path> and exit non-zero.' Add a verification line: grep -nF 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context' commands/commit.md returns >=1 match.

objections.title: AC4 verification recipe is time-transient and will produce a false-pass once any future spec acquires the marker
objections.detail: AC4's verification recipe reads: 'grep -rF spec-continuation-of docs/dev/specs/ returns no matches in pre-existing spec files (only future marker-bearing files would appear). Absence of marker in pre-existing files is confirmed.' This is correct TODAY (grep -rF returns exit 1, zero matches confirmed by QA), but the recipe is written against the entire docs/dev/specs/ directory. Once any future spec file contains the marker — which is the intended behavior of this very fix — the grep will return matches and the recipe as written will appear to fail, even though the pre-existing false-positive files are correctly excluded. The AC's intent is to verify that the known false-positive files (spec-20260518-225715.md, spec-20260520-221059.md) do not contain the marker for the relevant task-id. The verification recipe does not encode this intent precisely.
objections.evidence: AC4 verification text in ticket-20260522-080646-C.md lines 157-163. QA independently confirmed: grep -rF 'spec-continuation-of' docs/dev/specs/ exits 1 today (no matches). This state is transient. The AC4 verification recipe as written cannot remain valid after any correct spec ships the marker.
objections.proposed_fix: Rewrite the AC4 verification recipe to target the known pre-existing false-positive files with the relevant task-id: 'grep -lF <!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 --> docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md returns empty (exit 1 — no matches).' This is equivalent to AC1's verification recipe and is stable regardless of future spec additions.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Marker-placement obligation for future spec authors is not enforced by any mechanism. Stage 3 will silently produce empty candidate sets for task-ids where the spec author forgot to add the marker. BA acknowledges this as future-cycle debt (Edge Cases section). Codex also flagged this as OBSERVATION_ONLY. Not a blocker for this cycle.

The BA spec references 'line 157' as the change location. Line numbers in Markdown command files can drift if other lines are inserted before this location during development. However, the content predicate itself (the grep fragment) is a stable anchor for locating the change. Codex flagged this as OBSERVATION_ONLY. Not a blocker.

findings: BA claims commands/commit.md line 157 contains 'grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md' — QA confirmed this by direct read; the exact text is present.

BA claims the incident occurred in cycle 20260519-211515 with two false-positive specs — QA confirmed spec-20260518-225715.md has 3 prose references at lines 233, 235, 245; spec-20260520-221059.md has 7 prose references at lines 87, 90, 91, 132, 133, 134, 181.
BA claims spec-20260520-221059.md:87 documents the incident as 'D2 — Step 7 false positive' — QA confirmed the exact text at that line.
BA claims neither false-positive file contains a spec-continuation-of marker — QA confirmed via grep -cF returning 0 for both files.

BA's evidence_type is listed as 'inferred' in context JSON, but the observations[] array shows direct reads and measured grep counts — minor label mismatch but not a blocker since the observations are factual.

User requirement (verbatim from user-requirement doc): 'C — commit.md Step 7 ambiguous spec candidate. Stage 3 uses a literal grep of the task-id string across all docs/dev/specs/*.md files, which returns false positives. The fix is to tighten the candidate predicate: only a spec file that contains a machine-readable marker should be a candidate.'
BA scopes the change to ONE grep pattern replacement at ONE location (commands/commit.md:157). No other files, no other lines.
BA's Won't Have list correctly excludes: stage 1, stage 2, fail-closed policy, retroactive marker injection, any other file.
No universal scope claims ('all', 'every') in the user requirement that BA has narrowed — the requirement is already scoped to a single component.
The marker format `<!-- spec-continuation-of: <task-id> -->` is preserved verbatim from user requirement text.
BA DID investigate: structural separation of stage 3 predicate from stage 4 outcome — confirmed these are in separate numbered paragraphs (3) and (4) in commit.md.
BA DID investigate: whether changing the predicate breaks the multi-candidate fail-closed path — confirmed via AC3.
BA DID investigate: retroactive compatibility — confirmed no existing spec has the marker, addressed gracefully via AC4.

BA DID NOT investigate: whether the single-candidate fail-closed path (exactly one element → exit non-zero) is also preserved. Stage 4 has two distinct fail-closed branches; BA's AC3 covers only the multi-candidate branch. See OBJ-1.
commands/commit.md exists at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/commands/commit.md.
Step 7 stage-3 (labeled '(3) Mtime + literal-task-id glob (final stage).') is at lines 156-157.
The grep predicate 'grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md' is confirmed at line 157, exactly as BA claims.
Only ONE occurrence of grep.*TASK_ID exists in the entire commit.md file — BA's scope claim that this is the only affected location is confirmed.
The fail-closed multi-candidate text is confirmed at line 162: 'multiple specs produced this cycle without context linkage: <paths>; explicit context.spec_path required'.
The single-candidate fail-closed text is confirmed at line 161: 'spec produced this cycle but not linked in context: <path>'.

AC4's verification recipe uses a directory-wide grep ('grep -rF spec-continuation-of docs/dev/specs/') that is correct today but becomes stale once any future spec correctly carries the marker. The recipe's intent is to verify the known false-positive files are excluded — it should be written to target those files specifically.
AC1's verification recipe is correctly targeted: it checks the two known false-positive files with the specific task-id. AC4 should follow the same pattern.

The marker format itself is mechanically correct: `grep -lF '<!-- spec-continuation-of: 20260519-211515 -->'` empirically matches the marker and does not match prose references — verified via stdin test. No drift on the marker format.
AC5 verification recipe is correct: grep -nF 'literal-task-id grep' returns 0; grep -nE 'marker grep|machine-readable marker' returns >=1. No drift detected on AC5.

technical_verification.commit_md_line_157_content: confirmed — exact text: 'run grep -lF "${TASK_ID}" candidate.md — this is the ONLY content predicate allowed; no other grep, no free-form content scan'

summary.notes: BA's core analysis is technically accurate and the fix is correctly scoped. Two AC coverage gaps must be addressed before dev proceeds: OBJ-1 (AC3 misses single-candidate fail-closed branch) and OBJ-2 (AC4 verification recipe will drift once any valid spec ships the marker). Both require only wording changes to the BA spec — no change to the proposed fix itself.

failed_criteria: AC3 verification recipe covers only multi-candidate fail-closed path; single-candidate path (line 161) not included
AC4 verification recipe uses directory-wide grep that will produce false-pass once any future spec has the marker
refined_context.recommended_approach: BA should: (1) extend AC3 to add a sub-clause and verification for the single-candidate outcome at commit.md line 161; (2) rewrite AC4's verification recipe to grep the specific known false-positive files with the specific task-id marker (mirroring AC1's verification pattern). The proposed fix itself (the grep predicate replacement) is correct and requires no change.
refined_context.additional_context: The fix is sound. Only the AC coverage in the BA spec needs tightening. Once BA updates OBJ-1 and OBJ-2, this should proceed directly to dev.
